Salvage.BannerNoCheckRule: Breaking this down always recovers the materials below. No roll is needed.

Salvage.MisconfiguredProgressive: This salvage spends a roll down its results, but your GM hasn't set a roll formula for it yet. Ask them to finish configuring it.
Salvage.FooterNote: Nothing to roll — breaking this down consumes one of the item and recovers the full set.
Salvage.FooterNoteRoll: One roll, one shot — rolling breaks the item down immediately. No reroll.
Salvage.Ribbon: Salvaged — materials added to your pack
Salvage.Depleted: That was the last one — nothing left to salvage.

RepairComponentSources.Body: This scans your world items, unlocked compendiums, and actor inventories, tags each component and recipe-item source with a durable identity link, clears misleading duplicate-source metadata, and re-points owned copies that a duplicate mislabelled. Locked (system/module) compendiums are skipped. Continue?

RepairComponentSources.Repointed: {repointed} owned copies were re-pointed to the book or scroll their name matches. See the console log for the reversible details.
RepairComponentSources.Failed: Item data repair failed. See the console for details.

CraftingTabs.Label: Crafting sections
CraftingTabs.SettingsHint: System-level crafting rules: resolution mode and recipe visibility.

Settings.ResolutionIntro: Choose how a crafting attempt is turned into a result. Applies to every recipe in the system.
Settings.SalvageIntro: Choose how a salvage attempt is turned into returned components.
Settings.Subtitle: Control how players get access to the recipes in this system.
Settings.VisibilityIntro: Pick how a recipe becomes available to a character. Exactly one mode is active for the whole system.
Settings.VisibilityIntroAlchemy: Pick how a recipe is revealed in a character’s Known recipes list. Any character can still brew any recipe by matching its ingredients.

Visibility.GlobalDesc: Every player and character sees all recipes. Nothing is gated.
Visibility.GlobalAlchemyDesc: Every recipe a character brews is revealed to them. Brewing is never gated.
Visibility.ItemDesc: A character must have the item in their pack. Only mode where limited use appears in the Books & Scrolls editor.
Visibility.ItemAlchemyDesc: A recipe is revealed while a character holds its linked book or scroll. Brewing is never gated.
Visibility.KnowledgeDesc: A character must learn the recipe from a scroll or book using the item. Only mode where learning limits appear in the Books & Scrolls editor.
Visibility.KnowledgeAlchemyDesc: A recipe is revealed once a character learns it from a book or scroll. Brewing is never gated.
Visibility.RestrictedDesc: Recipes can be made visible to specific characters or players.
Visibility.ManualDesc: You reveal recipes to specific characters or players in the Access tab. Any character can still brew any recipe by matching its ingredients.

Effect.SummaryGlobal: Every recipe is visible to all players.
Effect.SummaryRestricted: Grant recipes to specific characters or players in the Access tab.
Effect.SummaryItem: Players craft from recipes only while holding the linked item. Configure usage limits in the Books & Scrolls editor.
Effect.SummaryKnowledge: Players learn recipes by reading books and scrolls. Configure learning limits in the Books & Scrolls editor.

BooksScrolls.Title: Books & Scrolls

Checks.Title: Checks
Checks.Subtitle: Configure how crafting, salvage, and gathering attempts are checked for the selected crafting system.

Active.Title: Active
Active.OptionalHint: Turn this check on to require a roll for the activity, or off to resolve it without one.
Active.RequiredHint: The current resolution mode requires this check, so it cannot be turned off here.
Active.GatheringHint: In d100 mode the gathering check is the fixed d100 roll and cannot be turned off here.
Active.AlchemyNoneHint: This alchemy system resolves without a crafting check. Choose Simple or Tiered at the top of the Crafting tab to author one.

Crafting.PageLead: A system has a single crafting check. Its shape is determined by the system's resolution mode and preserved when you switch modes.
Crafting.ConfigHint: Roll, difficulty, and outcome settings for the crafting check will appear here.
Crafting.AlchemyNoneLead: This alchemy system is set to “No check”, so a matched brew always succeeds and produces its single result set. There is nothing to configure here. Choose Simple or Tiered above to author a crafting check.
Crafting.HelpDesc: The crafting check is shaped by the system resolution mode: simple authors a pass/fail check, routed authors outcome tiers, and progressive requires a check. Alchemy is driven by its check mode — none has no check, simple authors a mandatory pass/fail check, and tiered authors a mandatory routed check.
Crafting.TypeRelativeDesc: Tier thresholds are relative to the recipe DC, e.g. DC -5 or DC +10.
Crafting.TypeFixedDesc: Each tier owns a non-overlapping segment of the roll value range. Recipes do not have a DC in this mode.
Crafting.NoDice: No dice detected in this expression.
Crafting.NoOutcomes: No outcome tiers yet. Add the tiers this check routes results into.
Crafting.RangeInvalid: Some tiers have a start greater than their end.
Crafting.RangeOverlap: Some tier ranges overlap. Each value range must be unique.
Crafting.OutcomeUnnamed: Name every outcome tier — an unnamed tier cannot be routed to a result group.
Crafting.OutcomeNoSuccess: No outcome tier is marked as a Success — successful crafts can't route to a result set. Mark at least one tier as Success.
Crafting.ProgressiveLead: Roll a formula for a numeric value. Results are awarded in order, each spending its difficulty from the value, until the value can no longer cover the next. Per-die crits force award-all or award-none.
Crafting.AwardEqualDesc: Award each result in order while the value covers its full difficulty.
Crafting.AwardPartialDesc: Like equal, but the first result the value can't fully cover is still awarded, then awarding stops.
Crafting.AwardExceedDesc: Award each result in order only while the value strictly exceeds its difficulty.
Crafting.CritMultiDieHint: The value is the {die} total, from {min} to {max}.
Crafting.CritModifiedPoolHint: Critical rolls match a die's total. They aren't available for pools that keep, drop, explode, or reroll dice (e.g. 2d20kh1).
Crafting.DcStaticDesc: A fixed DC for every recipe, with optional named recipe tiers.
Crafting.DcDynamicDesc: A macro computes the DC from the ingredients, recipe, and actor.
Crafting.NoTiers: No tiers yet. Add named tiers a recipe can select to override the DC.
Crafting.MacroHint: The macro is run with the selected ingredient set, the recipe, and the actor, and must return the DC.
Crafting.MacroDropHint: Drag a macro here to compute the DC.

Salvage.PageLead: A system has a single salvage check. Its shape is determined by the system's salvage resolution mode and preserved when you switch modes.
Salvage.ConfigHint: Roll, difficulty, and outcome settings for the salvage check will appear here.
Salvage.ProgressiveLead: Progressive salvage spends the check value against each salvage result's difficulty, in order. The award mode decides how that spend stops.
Salvage.HelpDesc: The salvage check is shaped by the salvage resolution mode. Simple mode makes it optional; routed and progressive modes require it.

Gathering.PageLead: A system has a single gathering check. In d100 mode it is the fixed d100 roll and is not editable; progressive and routed modes let you define it. Per-task tuning adjusts the difficulty, not the roll.
Gathering.ConfigHint: Roll, difficulty, and outcome settings for the gathering check will appear here.
Gathering.D100Lead: In d100 mode the gathering check is a fixed d100 roll against each drop's chance. There is nothing to configure here.
Gathering.D100Hint: Switch the gathering economy to progressive or routed resolution to define an editable check. Per-task tuning adjusts difficulty, not the roll.
Gathering.HelpDesc: The gathering check is shaped by the gathering resolution mode. In d100 mode it is the fixed d100 roll; progressive and routed modes let you define it.

Validation.IssueNoSuccessOutcome: No outcome tier is marked as a Success — successful crafts can't route to a result set. Mark at least one tier as Success.
Validation.IssueRangeInvalid: Some tiers have a start greater than their end.
Validation.IssueRangeOverlap: Some tier ranges overlap. Each value range must be unique.

SystemSettings.Name: Name

SystemSettings.ResolutionSimpleDesc: One ingredient set and one result group, with an optional pass/fail check.
SystemSettings.ResolutionRoutedDesc: Multiple ingredient sets and result groups; exactly one result is selected per craft, routed by the crafting check outcome or by the ingredients used.
SystemSettings.ResolutionRoutedByIngredientsDesc: Multiple ingredient sets and result groups; the chosen ingredient set selects which result group is produced. The crafting check is optional.
SystemSettings.ResolutionRoutedByCheckDesc: Multiple ingredient sets and result groups; the crafting check outcome selects which result group is produced. Requires a crafting check.
SystemSettings.ResolutionProgressiveDesc: One ingredient set and one ordered result group. A numeric check awards every result whose difficulty threshold is met.
SystemSettings.ResolutionAlchemyDesc: Players submit ingredient combinations directly to discover hidden recipes; one result is selected per attempt.

SystemSettings.SalvageResolutionSimpleDesc: One result group, with an optional pass/fail salvage check.
SystemSettings.SalvageResolutionProgressiveDesc: One ordered result group; a numeric salvage check awards every result whose difficulty threshold is met.
SystemSettings.SalvageResolutionRoutedDesc: Multiple result groups; the salvage check outcome selects which result group is returned.
SystemSettings.SalvageResolutionModeChangeContent: Changing the salvage resolution mode for {name}: components incompatible with the new salvage mode will have salvage disabled.

Alchemy.CheckModeIntro: Choose how a matched brew is resolved: with no check, a simple pass/fail check, or a tiered routed check.

Alchemy.CheckModeSimpleDesc: A mandatory pass/fail check. On a pass the success result set is produced; on a fail the reserved failure result set is.
Alchemy.CheckModeTieredDesc: A mandatory routed check. Each success outcome tier routes to its assigned result set, exactly like routed-by-check.
